name: gitignore-and-config
title: Add .gitignore and appsettings.json configuration
prompt:
This is the ClaudeCodeOrchestrator project in src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/. Do two things:

1. Create a .gitignore at the repo root for .NET projects (bin/, obj/, *.user, .vs/, etc.). Also ignore .claude/logs/.

2. The connection string is hardcoded in Program.cs (line 5) and in Data/OrchestratorDbContextFactory.cs (line 15). Refactor to use an appsettings.json file:
   - Create src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/appsettings.json with a ConnectionStrings section (key 'Default') and an 'Orchestrator' section with keys: MaxBudgetUsd (double, default 5.0), BuildCommand (string, default 'dotnet build'), MaxTurns (int, default 50), AllowedTools (string, default 'Read Write Edit Bash Glob Grep').
   - In the .csproj, add Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration and Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.Json NuGet packages.
   - In Program.cs, load configuration from appsettings.json using ConfigurationBuilder, read the connection string from config, and pass relevant settings to services.
   - Update OrchestratorDbContextFactory to also read from appsettings.json.
   - Make sure 'dotnet build' passes after your changes. Commit everything.

name: fix-prompt-passing
title: Fix AgentRunner to pass prompts via temp file instead of CLI arg
prompt:
In src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/Services/AgentRunner.cs, the RunClaudeProcessAsync method passes the prompt as a -p CLI argument (line 113). This breaks on long prompts due to shell argument length limits.

Fix this:
1. Instead of passing the prompt via -p "...", write the prompt to a temp file and use the --prompt-file flag: claude --prompt-file <tempfile> --allowedTools ... --max-turns ... --max-budget-usd ...
   NOTE: If --prompt-file doesn't exist in claude CLI, use stdin instead: pipe the prompt into claude's stdin with the -p - flag, or simply use Process.StandardInput to write the prompt and close stdin.
2. Actually, the simplest approach: write the prompt to a temp .txt file, then pass it via: claude -p "$(cat tempfile)" won't work either. Instead, use Process.StartInfo to redirect StandardInput, write the prompt to stdin, then close it. The claude CLI reads from stdin when no -p is given. Test by checking that the process still gets the prompt.
3. Clean up the temp resources after the process exits.
4. Remove the EscapeForShell method since it's no longer needed.
5. Also: read the MaxTurns, MaxBudget, and AllowedTools from constructor parameters instead of hardcoding them. Update the AgentRunner constructor to accept these (they were added to appsettings.json in the previous step). Update Program.cs to pass them from configuration.

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: plan-reload-and-build-cmd
title: Add --reload flag and configurable build command
prompt:
In the ClaudeCodeOrchestrator project (src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/):

1. PlanLoader (Services/PlanLoader.cs): The LoadOrGetPlanAsync method returns the existing plan from DB if SourceFile matches, even if the JSON file has changed. Add a 'forceReload' parameter (default false). When true, delete the existing plan and its steps from DB, then reimport from the JSON file. This enables a --reload CLI flag.

2. Program.cs: Add a --reload CLI flag that sets forceReload=true when calling loader.LoadOrGetPlanAsync.

3. BuildChecker (Services/BuildChecker.cs): The build command is hardcoded to 'dotnet build'. Change the constructor to accept a buildCommand string parameter. In CheckBuildAsync, split the buildCommand into filename and arguments (first token = filename, rest = arguments). Update Program.cs to read the BuildCommand from configuration and pass it to BuildChecker.

4. While you're at it, also update Orchestrator.cs line 100 — the auto-fix prompt is in German. Change it to English: 'The build failed. Run the build command, read the errors, and fix all compile errors. Commit the fix.'

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: english-prompts
title: Switch AgentRunner hardcoded prompts to English
prompt:
In src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/Services/AgentRunner.cs, the BuildPrompt method (around line 84) has German instructions ('Arbeite NUR den unten beschriebenen Schritt ab', etc.). Replace them with English equivalents:
- 'Work ONLY on the step described below, nothing more.'
- 'Make sure the build passes when you are done.'
- 'Commit your changes with a descriptive commit message.'
- 'If you encounter problems, fix them independently.'

Also check Orchestrator.cs for any remaining German text and translate it to English.

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: spectre-console-output
title: Replace Console.WriteLine with Spectre.Console rich output
prompt:
The project already depends on Spectre.Console (see ClaudeCodeOrchestrator.csproj). Replace all plain Console.WriteLine calls in Program.cs with Spectre.Console equivalents:

1. The startup banner (plan name, phase info, budget) should use a Spectre Panel with a nice border.
2. Step headers (OnStepStarted) should use AnsiConsole.MarkupLine with bold/colored text using Spectre markup like [bold cyan]...[/].
3. Success messages (OnStepCompleted) should use [green]...[/].
4. Failure messages (OnStepFailed) should use [bold red]...[/].
5. Build check messages should use [yellow]...[/] for 'checking' and [green]/[red] for results.
6. The progress summary at the end should be rendered as a Spectre Table with columns: Status, Step, Title, Duration, Files Changed. Color the Status column based on the status value.
7. Info messages should use [dim]...[/].

Do NOT add a live spinner yet (that's a separate step). Just replace the static output.

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: live-status-spinner
title: Add Spectre.Console live status spinner during agent execution
prompt:
In src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/Program.cs, add a Spectre.Console StatusContext (AnsiConsole.Status()) that shows a spinner while each agent step is running.

The flow should be:
- When OnStepStarted fires, update the status text to show which step is running.
- When OnOutput fires, show the last line of output below the spinner (use ctx.Status() to update).
- When OnBuildCheckStarted fires, update status to 'Checking build...'.
- When OnStepCompleted/OnStepFailed fires, the status ends.

Since AnsiConsole.Status() is synchronous and wraps an action, you may need to restructure the execution slightly. One approach: wrap the orchestrator.ExecutePhaseAsync call inside AnsiConsole.Status().StartAsync(), and use the StatusContext to update text during callbacks.

Alternatively, use AnsiConsole.Progress() with a single task that updates as steps complete.

Pick whichever approach is cleaner. Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: list-and-progress-commands
title: Add --list and --progress subcommands
prompt:
In src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/Program.cs, add two new CLI modes:

1. --list: Load the plan from the JSON file (via PlanLoader), then print all phases and steps as a Spectre.Console Tree or Table. Show phase number, step number, name, title, and a truncated prompt (first 80 chars). Then exit without executing anything.

2. --progress: Load the plan, then call orchestrator.GetProgressAsync() and display the result as a Spectre.Console Table. Columns: Phase, Step, Name, Status, Attempts, Duration, Files. Color-code the Status column. Then exit.

For both modes, parse them as flags in the existing CLI arg parser. If --list is set, show the list and return 0. If --progress is set, show progress and return 0. Otherwise proceed with normal execution.

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: help-and-version
title: Add --help and --version flags
prompt:
In src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/Program.cs, add:

1. --help or -h: Print a usage message showing all available flags and their descriptions, then exit 0. Use AnsiConsole.Write with a Spectre Panel for nice formatting. Include examples like:
   dotnet run -- --plan todos.json --phase 1
   dotnet run -- --phase 1 --step 3
   dotnet run -- --list
   dotnet run -- --progress
   dotnet run -- --dry-run --phase 1
   dotnet run -- --reload --phase 1

2. --version or -v: Print 'ClaudeCodeOrchestrator v0.1.0' and exit 0. Read the version from the assembly (or just hardcode '0.1.0' for now).

Parse these before other flags so they short-circuit execution.

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: context-skip-markdone
title: Add --context, --skip, and --mark-done CLI flags
prompt:
In src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator/Program.cs, add three new CLI flags:

1. --context <text>: A context preamble string that gets passed to orchestrator.ExecutePhaseAsync as the contextPreamble parameter. This lets the user provide project-specific context that gets prepended to every agent prompt.

2. --skip <step>: Mark a specific step (by step number within the current phase) as Skipped in the database without running it. Add a 'Skipped' value to the AgentStatus enum in Models/AgentStatus.cs. Create a new AgentRun with Status=Skipped for that step. Print confirmation and exit.

3. --mark-done <step>: Mark a specific step as Succeeded without running it (useful when you completed a step manually). Create a new AgentRun with Status=Succeeded. Print confirmation and exit.

For --skip and --mark-done, you need to load the plan first to find the step, then create the AgentRun record. Don't forget to add a new EF migration if the enum change affects the database schema (it shouldn't since EF stores enums as ints by default, but add one if needed).

Make sure 'dotnet build' passes. Commit.

name: test-project-setup
title: Add xUnit test project with core service tests
prompt:
Create a test project for the orchestrator:

1. Create src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator.Tests/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator.Tests.csproj as an xUnit test project targeting net9.0. Add references to the main project, plus NuGet packages: xunit, xunit.runner.visualstudio, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk, and Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory (for in-memory DB testing).

2. Create a solution file at the repo root: dotnet new sln, then add both projects.

3. Write tests in src/ClaudeCodeOrchestrator.Tests/Services/:

   a. PlanLoaderTests.cs: Test that LoadOrGetPlanAsync correctly deserializes a todos.json file into Plan + PlanSteps. Test the forceReload parameter. Use EF InMemory provider.

   b. OrchestratorTests.cs: Test ExecutePhaseAsync with a mock/fake AgentRunner (you'll need to extract an IAgentRunner interface from AgentRunner and have Orchestrator depend on the interface). Test: dry run mode returns success, steps execute in order, build failure triggers auto-fix. Use EF InMemory provider.

   c. BuildCheckerTests.cs: A simple test that BuildChecker can be constructed and that CheckBuildAsync runs without crashing (integration-style test that runs 'dotnet --version' as the build command).

4. Extract IAgentRunner interface from AgentRunner (in Services/IAgentRunner.cs) so Orchestrator can be tested with a fake. Update Orchestrator constructor to accept IAgentRunner.

Run 'dotnet test' to verify all tests pass. Commit.
